rolling_30d.summary: Pull-A-Part's local pages experienced modest MoM growth (+2.6% sessions, +5.1% engaged sessions) but faces a severe YoY headwind, with total sessions down 45.0% and engaged sessions down 75.1% versus the same period last year. Direct traffic remains the dominant channel at 76.9% of sessions and grew 167.2% YoY, suggesting strong brand recall and repeat visitation, yet this growth masks alarming declines in organic search (-86.4% YoY) and paid/referral channels. High-value conversions show mixed signals: calls grew 11.9% MoM (+188 events) but plummeted 81.7% YoY, while outbound_clicks held flat MoM but fell 73.2% YoY. The Harvey, Louisiana location (/louisiana/harvey/4401-peters-rd) remains the engagement leader at 69.2%, but secondary markets show inconsistent traction and zero engagement. YTD 2026 tracking at -38.9% versus 2025 across Q1, with February representing the sharpest monthly decline (-45.6%), signals a structural traffic loss rather than seasonal volatility.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on Harvey, Louisiana momentum
quick_wins.detail: The /louisiana/harvey/4401-peters-rd page generated 548 sessions with 69.2% engagement rate—highest among all location pages. Replicate this landing page structure and conversion flow to secondary locations (Charlotte, Greensboro, Foley) that show lower engagement (0–45%).

quick_wins.title: Double down on Direct channel strength
quick_wins.detail: Direct traffic is 76.9% of volume and grew 167.2% YoY, indicating strong brand equity. Invest in retargeting and email nurture to convert repeat visitors into high-value calls and mainsite_clicks, currently underperforming relative to session volume.

quick_wins.title: Leverage 'locate_me' momentum
quick_wins.detail: The locate_me event grew 25.0% MoM (35 events) despite overall decline, suggesting mobile-first location intent. Optimize this feature's visibility and conversion path to calls or store_locate actions.

potential_warnings.title: Catastrophic YoY organic search decline
potential_warnings.detail: Organic search traffic dropped 86.4% YoY to just 1,130 sessions (20.7% of total). This suggests significant visibility loss in search results, likely due to algorithm changes, lost backlinks, or on-page SEO degradation—requires immediate technical and content audit.

potential_warnings.title: Severe engagement cliff in secondary markets
potential_warnings.detail: Multiple Ohio, North Carolina, and South Carolina location pages (Newbury, Litchfield, Columbia, Medina) show 0% engagement or single-digit sessions. These pages are traffic drains and may indicate missing inventory, outdated information, or poor local SEO setup.

potential_warnings.title: Year-over-year conversion collapse across all high-value events
potential_warnings.detail: Calls are down 81.7% YoY, outbound_clicks down 73.2% YoY, and navigational_clicks down 79.3% YoY. Combined with -45.0% session decline YoY, this indicates both traffic loss and conversion quality degradation—not just volume contraction.

potential_warnings.title: Navigational clicks plummeting MoM
potential_warnings.detail: Navigational_clicks fell 34.9% MoM (127 events) despite slight session growth, suggesting users are less likely to explore the site or move between pages—possible UX friction or content relevance issue.

biggest_opportunities.title: Recover organic search visibility
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search collapsed -86.4% YoY; even modest recovery to 50% of prior-year volume would add ~500+ sessions monthly. Conduct full technical SEO audit, rebuild local search citations, and align location page content with high-intent keywords (e.g., 'auto parts near me,' 'salvage yards').

biggest_opportunities.title: Improve secondary market location pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Charlotte (/north-carolina/charlotte/6024-n-tryon-st) dropped 27.9% MoM and Greensboro shows 15% engagement; audit inventory accuracy, hours, phone numbers, and implement templates proven by Harvey location's 69.2% engagement to standardize conversion funnels.

biggest_opportunities.title: Convert direct traffic to high-value calls
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct channel is 76.9% of volume (4,197 sessions) but calls only total 188—a 4.5% implicit conversion rate. Test prominent click-to-call CTAs, sticky phone headers, and landing page call button placement to unlock conversion upside on existing traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Activate store locator as conversion funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: Store_locate (62 events) and locate_me (35 events) combined represent ~100 high-intent interactions, but downstream conversion to calls is unclear. Map these events to downstream call/booking funnels and test UX improvements (one-click calling, map preview expansion) to close the gap.

last_calendar_month.summary: March 2026 delivered modest month-over-month growth with 5,674 sessions (+14.6% MoM) and 1,397 engaged sessions (+21.4% MoM), suggesting stabilization after February's sharp decline. However, YoY performance remains deeply challenged: sessions are down 45.1%, engaged sessions down 74.7%, and the engagement rate has contracted 28.7 percentage points despite improving MoM. High-value conversions show mixed signals—calls grew 22.0% MoM but collapsed 81.4% YoY, while the critical store_locate event (+18.0% MoM) remains 42.4% below March 2025. Direct traffic dominates at 76.7% of sessions (+164.2% YoY), an unusual strength that masks organic search's severe 86.3% YoY decline. Q1 2026 YTD sits at 18,130 sessions versus 29,678 in 2025 (−38.9%), with February marking the low point before modest March recovery. The Louisiana Harvey location (579 sessions, 69.8% engagement) is the only high-traffic page showing strong engagement, while most other location pages lag with engagement rates below 45%. Without urgent intervention to restore organic visibility and rebuild YoY conversion volume, the downward trajectory will likely persist into Q2.

quick_wins.title: Direct Traffic Surge as Retention Lever
quick_wins.detail: Direct channel grew 164.2% YoY and now represents 76.7% of all traffic (4,352 sessions in March). Capitalize on this brand loyalty by implementing retargeting campaigns and email nurture sequences to convert repeat visitors into location-specific engagement and calls.

quick_wins.title: Optimize Louisiana Harvey Location Page
quick_wins.detail: The /louisiana/harvey/4401-peters-rd page delivers 69.8% engagement rate (404 engaged of 579 sessions) and grew 13.3% MoM—far outpacing site average of 24.6%. Audit and replicate its UX, CTA placement, and content structure across underperforming location pages.

quick_wins.title: Calls Recovery Momentum
quick_wins.detail: Phone calls rebounded 22.0% MoM (194 events in March), signaling renewed conversion intent despite YoY headwinds. Double down on call-to-action prominence and local SEO to sustain this trend into Q2.

potential_warnings.title: Catastrophic YoY Decline in High-Value Conversions
potential_warnings.detail: Calls plummeted 81.4% YoY (194 vs. ~1,050 estimated), and outbound_clicks dropped 72.7% YoY (2,340 vs. ~8,600 estimated). This signals severe erosion of conversion funnel quality and buyer intent, not just traffic volume.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Search Collapse
potential_warnings.detail: Organic search sessions fell 86.3% YoY (1,175 vs. ~8,600 estimated) while making up only 20.7% of March traffic. Without recovery in organic visibility and rankings, the site is increasingly dependent on direct traffic, which is unsustainable.

potential_warnings.title: Q1 YTD Trajectory Below Prior Year
potential_warnings.detail: Q1 2026 closed at 18,130 sessions versus 29,678 in 2025 (−38.9%), with February representing the lowest point at 4,953 sessions. While March shows recovery, the quarterly performance gap signals systemic headwinds that month-to-month bounces cannot mask.

potential_warnings.title: Low-Intent Engagement Dominance
potential_warnings.detail: Navigational_clicks represent only 148 events but fell 75.7% YoY, while store_locate (72 events, −42.4% YoY) and high-value appointment actions are absent from the event list. The site is capturing clicks but not intent-driven location or booking signals.

potential_warnings.title: Geographic Concentration Risk
potential_warnings.detail: Two locations (Louisiana Harvey and North Carolina Charlotte) account for the majority of engaged traffic; most other location pages show 0–15% engagement rates. Underperforming locations require immediate audit and localization optimization.

biggest_opportunities.title: Restore Organic Search Visibility
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search collapsed 86.3% YoY and now drives only 20.7% of traffic. Conduct a technical and content SEO audit focusing on location-based keywords, local schema markup, and competitor gap analysis to recapture organic volume and high-intent traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale and Replicate Louisiana Harvey UX Model
biggest_opportunities.detail: The /louisiana/harvey/4401-peters-rd page's 69.8% engagement rate is 2.8x the site average. Systematically audit its layout, CTAs, inventory presentation, and form fields, then standardize across 30+ underperforming location pages.

biggest_opportunities.title: Implement Location-Specific Booking/Scheduling Events
biggest_opportunities.detail: book_now and appointment events are entirely absent from the data despite pull-a-part's junkyard car sales model. Add location-specific scheduling CTAs and track them as conversion events to unlock visibility into the funnel and optimize checkout flow.

biggest_opportunities.title: Leverage Direct Traffic for Segment-Based Retargeting
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic is 76.7% of volume (+164.2% YoY) but only 24.6% engaged. Create segmented email and display campaigns targeting repeat direct visitors by location interest and engagement level to convert browsers into location visitors and callers.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate and Fix North Carolina Charlotte Decline
biggest_opportunities.detail: The Charlotte page (6024-n-tryon-st) dropped 88.5% YoY (32 sessions, −22.0% MoM) with only 43.8% engagement. Audit for technical issues, local ranking drops, or content staleness; this location may represent addressable revenue recovery.
